Item: Profile-panel sample size is sufficient for stable moderation estimates.
Mitigation: Interpret run_1 coefficients as exploratory panel diagnostics and scale the profile count before making substantive moderator claims.

Item: Attack-only design is intentional and aligned with the moderation question.
Mitigation: Interpret results as moderation among attacked individuals, not as a no-attack counterfactual contrast.

Item: Opinion measurement has enough numeric resolution.
Attack-generation quality checks indicate usable exposure texts.
Mitigation: Inspect Stage 03 reviewer outputs and treat zero-scored or fallback-generated attack content as a run-quality failure rather than as substantive evidence.

Item: Baseline and post-exposure opinions were generated without heavy fallback substitution.
Mitigation: Re-run stages 02–04 with funded API access before interpreting moderator coefficients as anything other than pipeline-diagnostic outputs.
Treat near-perfect fit in small profile panels as a saturation warning; rely on robustness diagnostics and larger panels instead of fit indices alone.

Item: LLM outputs are reproducible and auditable.
Synthetic LLM agents may not represent human cognition or causal response behavior.
Mitigation: Added transparent assumption register and explicit exploratory caveat in reports.

Item: Adversarial content may be unrealistic or too generic.
Mitigation: Added realism review agent with rewrite loop and heuristic checks.

Item: Model dependence on a single LLM could bias outputs.
Mitigation: Model is CLI-configurable and run metadata captures exact model for replication.

Item: Insufficient sample size inflates uncertainty and may destabilize SEM.
Mitigation: Included robust OLS complement and explicit testing-run warnings; ready for scale-up runs.

Item: Attack-only designs cannot estimate the incremental effect of exposure relative to a no-attack counterfactual.
Mitigation: The report states explicitly that the attacked-only design estimates heterogeneity of post-minus-baseline response among attacked individuals only.

Item: SEM warnings indicate possible misfit or numerical instability.
Mitigation: Warnings surfaced in reports and fit diagnostics exported for scrutiny.

Item: Global SEM fit is not evidential at this scale; either misfit or near-saturation can make fit indices misleading.
Mitigation: The report now treats SEM fit indices as diagnostics only and triangulates them with ICC, bootstrap, ridge, and fallback-quality diagnostics.
